subtopics.title: Factors Affecting Climate
common_misconceptions: Climate and weather are the same (weather is short-term, climate is long-term average)
Equatorial regions are always hot (also wet, with little temperature variation)
Deserts are always hot (cold deserts exist)
subtopics.teaching_note: Climate factors: Latitude (determines insolation angle), Altitude (lapse rate: 1°C per 165m), Distance from sea (maritime vs continental), Ocean currents (warm currents increase temperature, humidity), Prevailing winds, Relief (windward = wet, leeward = rain shadow). Nigerian climates: Equatorial (south - Cross River, Rivers, heavy rainfall year-round), Tropical monsoon (coastal), Tropical savanna (most of Nigeria - distinct wet/dry seasons), Sahel (north - semi-arid). Rainfall pattern: decreases northward (4000mm in south → 500mm in far north).

subtopics.title: Population Studies
common_misconceptions: High population density always means overpopulation
Census in Nigeria is accurate (challenges: politicization, nomadic populations)
subtopics.teaching_note: Nigerian context: Population ~220 million (2024), largest in Africa, 6th largest globally. Growth rate ~2.4% (one of highest). Population pyramid: broad base (young population, 40%+ under 15). Density: 240+/km² (2024), highly uneven (Lagos 3000+/km², Borno <100/km²). Census history: contentious, last credible attempt 2006. NPC (National Population Commission) conducts censuses. Challenges: youth bulge, unemployment, pressure on infrastructure. Demographic transition: Stage 2 (high birth rate, falling death rate). Population policies: National Population Policy (1988, revised).
